text: openalexR: Getting Bibliographic Records ‘OpenAlex’ Database Using ‘DSL’ API. rcrossref: R interface CrossRef APIs. rscopus: Scopus Database API Interface. wosr: Clients ‘Web Science’ ‘InCites’ APIs. rwos: R interface Web Science API Lite [ARCHIVED]. …

what: ropensci: Transforming science through open data and software
title: Bibliometric analysis with R
text: Selecting Literature filter ropensci/packages, list packages may useful field provided. Among (addition openalexR rcrossref) worth highlighting: aRxiv: Interface arXiv API. jstor: Import Analyse Data Scientific Articles. fulltext: Full Text ‘Scholarly’ Articles Across Many Data Sources. europepmc: R Interface Europe PubMed Central RESTful Web Service. IEEER: Interface IEEE Xplore Gateway [ARCHIVED].

title: Creación de la BD empleando la API de WoS (obsoleto)
text: https://github.com/YesicaFdzr/bibliometrics

title: Creación de la BD empleando la API de WoS (obsoleto)
text: Para la creación del sistema de información, es necesario tener instalado Python, concretamente, una versión igual o superior la 3.6. Importante que se encuentre en el path del sistema. Además, son necesarias las siguientes librerías: Pandas xml.etree.ElementTree sqlite3 re datetime Por otra parte, como la librería de WoS fue modificada en el proyecto, es importante descargar la carpeta WoS del repositorio y copiarla en la ruta donde se encuentra Python instalado, por ejemplo: C:/Python3.6/Lib/site-packages/. Además, se puede descargar DB Browser SQLite para inspeccionar los datos de la BD. Por último, se descargan los archivos del repositorio que se encuentran en la carpeta utils y actualizations.

title: Creación de la BD empleando la API de WoS (obsoleto)
text: Los scripts descargados pueden ejecutarse por separado. Es importante saber que para ejecutar aquellos que incorporan datos al modelo, es necesario contar con una base de datos creada. Para ello, existe un script, Creación_bd.py, al que sólo tenemos que pasarle el nombre que queremos ponerle al sistema de información y, si queremos que la cree en la ruta actual del archivo, la ruta de creación. Se muestran los dos ejemplos en la siguiente figura.  Para ejecutar los demás scripts el procedimiento es el mismo, cambiando los parámetros. La lista de parámetros necesarios para cada archivo se muestra en la siguiente tabla.

title: Creación de la BD empleando la API de WoS (obsoleto)
text: Para la creación de las actualizaciones únicamente es necesario cambiar las rutas en los archivos BAT. Adicionalmente, se pueden añadir en el programador de tareas de Windows las tareas necesarias. Para ello, en el repositorio existen tres archivos XML que se pueden importar directamente en el programador (ver figura siguiente). Una vez importados, sólo tendremos que cambiar la ruta del archivo BAT y los campos que creamos convenientes. Para más detalles ver Sección 4.7 de Fernández-Ramos (2021).

title: Creación de la BD empleando la API de WoS (obsoleto)
text: Bacis, Enrico (2019). wos: Web Science client using API v3. Python package version 0.2.5. https://github.com/enricobacis/wos. Fernández-Ramos, Yésica (2021). Análisis y Diseño de un Sistema de Información Bibliométrico de Publicaciones Científicas. Trabajo de fin de Grado en Ingeniería Informática.

code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("rubenfcasal/scimetr") # Dependencies install.packages(c("dplyr", "tidyr", "stringr", "ggplot2", "scales", "rlang", "openxlsx")) # Last released version install.packages("https://github.com/rubenfcasal/scimetr/releases/download/v1.2.0/scimetr_1.2.0.zip", repos = NULL)

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: summary_year() method breaks summary year, showing trends time publications, citations, key metrics.

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: ggplot2 package used create wide variety visualizations database. three main types plots can create: Database plots (plot(db)). Summary plots (plot(summary(db)). Yearly summary plots (plot(summary_year(db))). Note: plot() methods invisible return list generated ggplot2 objects (use plot = FALSE avoid plotting).

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: plot() method bibliographic database (plot.wos.db()) provides general visualization contents.

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: plot method summary result (plot.summary.wos.db()) visualizes results generating different types plots: standard bar, line plots Pie charts (pie = TRUE).

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: plot() method yearly summary (plot.summary.year()) visualizes results generating different types plots: standard bar line plots trends time, boxplots (boxplot = TRUE), show variability within year.

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: filter elements (entities) database, can use functions get_id_<table>() retrieve identification codes (IDs entity key values). variable corresponding <table> may used (multiple conditions combined &; see e.g. dplyr::filter()). Typically, functions combined together get_id_docs() function obtain document IDs, finally used argument filter summary functions filter documents results.

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: get_id_authors(): Retrieve author IDs (codes) Search specific author: Search partial name match: get_id_areas(): Retrieve codes research areas get_id_categories(): Retrieve category codes get_id_sources(): Retrieve source codes (journals, books, collections) Test journal
code: ida <- get_id_authors(db, AF == "Cao, Ricardo") ida ## Cao, Ricardo Cao, Ricardo  ##           11          260 idas <- get_id_authors(db, grepl("Cao", AF)) idas ##      Cao, Ricardo   Cao-Rial, M. T.      Cao, Ricardo           Cao, R.  ##                11                80               260               328  ## Cao Abad, Ricardo   Cao-Rial, M. T.  ##               501               504 get_id_areas(db, SC == "Mathematics") ## Mathematics  ##          16 get_id_areas(db, SC == "Mathematics" | SC == "Computer Science") ## Computer Science      Mathematics  ##                6               16 get_id_categories(db, grepl("Mathematics", WC)) ##                                 Mathematics  ##                                         148  ##                        Mathematics, Applied  ##                                         149  ## Mathematics, Interdisciplinary Applications  ##                                         150 idtest <- get_id_sources(db, SO == "TEST") idtest ## TEST  ##    6 knitr::kable(t(db$Sources[idtest, ]),   caption = "Test journal",   col.names = c("Variable", "Value") ) # get_id_sources(db, JI == 'Test')

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: Auxiliary functions available perform database queries: get_jcr(): combines document indexes source JCR metrics per year. get_jcr_cat() combines document indexes source JCR metrics per year WoS category (best = TRUE, results WoS category best ranking document returned). summary methods combine queries generate global summaries yearly summaries bibliographic database JCR metrics (= TRUE, corresponding wos.db-class results also generated). Note: res1$docjcrcat contains combined queries.

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: plot() method (plot.wos.jcr()) generates histograms documents JCR metrics (argument = TRUE, plot.wos.bd() also called):  Summary results also plot() method:

title: Introduction to the scimetr package
text: following list shows variables used database tables: Note: table generated using datatable() function DT package. default, first rows displayed. Click page index () change . can sort column clicking arrows right name. can search values (search box, top right) filter values (click filter box name variable).

what: Web of Science search web page
title: Downloading data from the Web of Science
text: Access Clarivate Web Science (WoS) search web page: https://www.webofscience.com/wos/ (institutional access link; instance https://www.recursoscientificos.fecyt.es Spanish Foundation Science Technology, FECYT Consortium Academic Group). Select Web Science Core Collection database top dropdown menu Search , choose desired options: Editions: dropdown menu can used select citation indexes (example, following IUNE criteria, first three select: Science Citation Index Expanded, Social Sciences Citation Index Arts & Humanities Citation Index). Add date range: limit results specific period. Add row: include additional search criteria. Query Builder: build complex queries using field tags Boolean operators. Note: Visit online training portal details: https://clarivate.com/academia-government/training-support/.    Set search fields click Search. want download scientific output linked university, can use Affiliation field (OG Advanced Search). can search specific name start typing search field. names universities make Galician University System (SUG) : UDC: Universidade da Coruna. USC: Universidade de Santiago de Compostela. UVIGO: Universidade de Vigo. Additionally, can specify year range.

title: Downloading data from the Web of Science
text: obtain results list, select Export Tab delimited file (need repeat number records exceeds 500, default download limit).    pop-window, set record range (considering 500-record limit , last step, enter exact total number records upper limit), make sure select Full Record Cited References Record Content field.    click Export, text file (savedrecs.txt default) containing publication data downloaded. recommended save files subdirectory, renaming appropriately.

what: Analysis of Scientific Publication Data with R
title: Analysis of Scientific Publication Data with R
text: package implements tools quantitative research scientometrics bibliometrics. provides routines importing bibliographic data Clarivate Web Science (https://www.webofscience.com/wos/) performing bibliometric analysis. information visit https://rubenfcasal.github.io/scimetr/articles/scimetr.html.

title: Analysis of Scientific Publication Data with R
text: scimetr available CRAN, can install development version github : Alternatively, Windows users may install corresponding scimetr_X.Y.Z.zip file releases section github repository.

what: Add JCR data to a bibliographic database — add_jcr
title: Add JCR data to a bibliographic database — add_jcr
text: Extends bibliographic database adding JCR metrics sources, per year WoS category.

title: Add JCR data to a bibliographic database — add_jcr
code: add_jcr(db, jcrdb)  # S3 method for class 'wos.jcr' plot(   x,   filter,   plot = TRUE,   all = FALSE,   ask = plot && all && interactive(),   ... )

title: Add JCR data to a bibliographic database — add_jcr
text: db bibliographic database (wos.db object; typically output function db_bib()). jcrdb JCR database (jcr.db object; typically output function db_jcr()). x wos.jcr object. filter vector document identifiers (usually result get_id_docs()). plot logical; TRUE (default), plots drawn, otherwise ggplot2 object (invisibly) returned. logical; TRUE, function plot.wos.db() called (additional parameters ... passed function), otherwise JCR metrics plot generated. ask logical; TRUE, user asked plot (see par(ask=.)). ... arguments passed methods.

title: Add JCR data to a bibliographic database — add_jcr
text: S3 object class wos.jcr. wos.db object additional components JCRSour JCRCatSour.

title: Mathematics UDC 2018-2023 bibliographic database — dbjcr
text: Bibliographic database JCR metrics (wos.jcr S3 object) corresponding WoS search Affiliation field Universidade da Coruña (UDC) research area "Mathematics" years 2018–2023 (generated fom wosdf data set, using functions db_bib, db_jcr add_jcr).

title: Mathematics UDC 2018-2023 bibliographic database — dbjcr
text: object class wos.jcr (inherits wos.db) length 22.

title: Bibliographic data base creation — db_bib
text: db_bib.wos.data() converts data.frame WoS variables (returned import_wos; see wosdf) relational database (list data.frames).

title: Bibliographic data base creation — db_bib
code: db_bib(data, ...)  # S3 method for class 'wos.data' db_bib(data, label = "", progress = interactive(), verbose = FALSE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'wos.db' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'wos.db' plot(   x,   filter,   which = 1:3,   plot = TRUE,   warning = FALSE,   ask = plot && (length(which) > 1) && interactive(),   ... )

title: Bibliographic data base creation — db_bib
text: data data.frame bibliographic data. ... arguments passed methods. label character string describing data. progress logical; TRUE progress printed. verbose logical; TRUE additional information printed. x object used select method. filter vector document identifiers (usually result get_id_docs). subset plots required, specify subset numbers 1:3. plot logical; TRUE (default), plots drawn, otherwise list ggplot2 objects (invisibly) returned. warning logical; FALSE (default), warnings ignored. ask logical; TRUE, user asked plot (see par(ask=.)).

title: Bibliographic data base creation — db_bib
text: S3 object class wos.db. list following components: Docs: document-level records (year, type, citations, identifiers). Authors AutDoc: author dictionary document-author links. OI OIDoc: ORCID identifiers linkage documents. RI RIDoc: ResearcherID identifiers linkage documents. Affiliations AffDoc: affiliation dictionary document links. Addresses AddAutDoc: address document-author-address links. Sources: document sources (journals, books, proceedings, ...). Categories, Areas, CatSour AreaSour: thematic classifications linkage sources. WSIndex SourWSI: Web Science indexes linkage sources. label date: metadata helps tracking dataset identity time. object specialized print plot methods.

title: Bibliographic data base creation — db_bib
code: db <- db_bib(wosdf) print(db) #> List of 20 #>  $ Docs        :'data.frame':	293 obs. of  28 variables: #>   ..- attr(*, "variable.labels")= Named chr [1:28] "Document ID" "Source ID" ".. #> what: Import JCR data from Web of Science (WoS) — db_jcr
title: Import JCR data from Web of Science (WoS) — db_jcr
text: Reads JCR data excel files downloaded WoS generates relational database (list data.frames). assumed file name format JCR_[]_[PY], [] WoS index (SCIE, SSCI, ...) [PY] JCR year.

title: Import JCR data from Web of Science (WoS) — db_jcr
code: db_jcr(path = ".", files = dir(path, pattern = "*.xlsx"), verbose = TRUE)

title: Import JCR data from Web of Science (WoS) — db_jcr
text: path character; path directory containing files. Defaults working directory. files character vector file names. Defaults filenames directory path extension .xlsx". verbose logical; indicating whether name file processed printed. Defaults TRUE.

title: Import JCR data from Web of Science (WoS) — db_jcr
text: S3 object class jcr.db. list components: Sources, Categories, JCRSour JCRCatSour.

title: Import JCR data from Web of Science (WoS) — db_jcr
text: subscription Web Science required download JCR data. successful execution, Excel files must retain original header fields Clarivate exports: Title20, ISO_ABBREV, TITLE, ISSN, EISSN, ISSUES/YEAR, COUNTRY, LANGUAGE, 1ST_YR_PUB, categories, TOT_CITES, CITES_JCR_YR, CITES_JCR_YR1, CITES_JCR_YR2, IF_NUMERATOR, CITES_JCR_YR3, CITES_JCR_YR4, CITES_JCR_YR5, 5YR_IF_NUMERATOR, ITEMS_JCR_YR, ITEMS_JCR_YR1, ITEMS_JCR_YR2, IF_DENOMINATOR, ITEMS_JCR_YR3, ITEMS_JCR_YR4, ITEMS_JCR_YR5, 5YR_IF_DENOMINATOR, IMPACT_FACTOR, IMMEDIACY_INDEX, CITED_HALF_LIFE, 5YR_IMPACT_FACTOR, EIGENFACTOR, NORM_EIGENFACTOR, ARTL_INFLUENCE, FREQUENCY, PUBCODE, PUBLISHER_NAME, CATEGORY_CODE, CATEGORY_DESCRIPTION, CATEGORY_RANKING, QUARTILE_RANK, JIF_PERCENTILE.

title: Get table identifiers — get_id_docs
text: Retrieve internal identifiers (entity keys) corresponding relational table (authors, sources, categories, areas, ...). field target table can used condition, multiple conditions combined & (IDs conditions evaluate TRUE returned). Tidy evaluation can used construct logical expressions, see dplyr::filter().

title: Get table identifiers — get_id_docs
code: get_id_docs(   db,   ...,   id_sources,   id_authors,   id_addresses,   id_oi,   id_ri,   id_affiliations )  get_id_authors(db, ...)  get_id_addresses(db, ...)  get_id_areas(db, ...)  get_id_categories(db, ...)  get_id_wsi(db, ...)  get_id_sources(db, ..., id_categories, id_areas, id_wsi)  get_id_oi(db, ...)  get_id_ri(db, ...)  get_id_affiliations(db, ...)

title: Get table identifiers — get_id_docs
text: db Object class wos.db, returned db_bib. ... Logical predicates. Multiple conditions combined & (see filter). id_sources optional; sources identifiers. id_authors optional; author identifiers (values db$Authors$ids). id_addresses optional; addresses identifiers. id_oi optional; ORCID identifiers codes (values db$OI$OI). id_ri optional; RI identifiers codes (values db$RI$RI). id_affiliations optional; affiliations identifiers names (values db$Affiliations$C3). id_categories optional; categories identifiers names (values db$Categories$WC) id_areas optional; research area identifiers names (values db$Areas$SC) id_wsi optional; WoS Index identifiers names (values db$WSI$)

title: Get table identifiers — get_id_docs
text: integer vector identifiers. instance, get_id_docs returns document identifiers (values db$Docs$idd variable) corresponding identifiers authors, categories, areas, addresses /sources. Logical expressions defined terms variables db$Docs can also used arguments.

title: Get JCR metrics — get_jcr
text: get_jcr() combines document indexes source JCR metrics per year. get_jcr_cat() combines document indexes source JCR metrics per year WoS category.

title: Get JCR metrics — get_jcr
code: get_jcr(db, filter)  get_jcr_cat(db, filter, best = TRUE)

title: Get JCR metrics — get_jcr
text: db bibliographic database JCR information (wos.jcr object; typically output function add_jcr()). filter vector document identifiers (usually result get_id_docs()). best logical; TRUE (default), results WoS category best ranking document returned.

title: Get JCR metrics — get_jcr
text: data.frame document indexes source JCR metrics.

what: Import bibliographic data downloaded from Web of Science (WoS). — import_wos
title: Import bibliographic data downloaded from Web of Science (WoS). — import_wos
text: Reads bibliography entries UTF-8 encoded Tab-delimited files containing "Full Record Cited References" (see wosdf vignette Downloading data Web Science).

title: Import bibliographic data downloaded from Web of Science (WoS). — import_wos
code: import_wos(path = ".", pattern = "*.txt", all = TRUE, progress = NULL)

title: Import bibliographic data downloaded from Web of Science (WoS). — import_wos
text: path character; path directory containing files. pattern regular expression; matching files loaded. Defaults "*.txt". logical; indicating whether sources without ISSN included (TRUE) (FALSE) result. Defaults TRUE. progress logical; indicating whether progress bar displayed. Defaults TRUE R used interactively one file, FALSE otherwise.

title: Import bibliographic data downloaded from Web of Science (WoS). — import_wos
text: data.frame rows corresponding sources columns WoS variables.

title: Import bibliographic data downloaded from Web of Science (WoS). — import_wos
text: subscription Web Science required download bibliometric data.

what: scimetr internal and secondary functions — scimetr-internals
title: scimetr internal and secondary functions — scimetr-internals
text: Listed supporting functions major methods scimetr.

title: scimetr internal and secondary functions — scimetr-internals

what: scimetr: Analysis of Scientific Publication Data with R — scimetr-package
title: scimetr: Analysis of Scientific Publication Data with R — scimetr-package
text: Tools quantitative research scientometrics bibliometrics. package provides routines importing bibliographic data Clarivate Web Science (https://www.webofscience.com/wos/) performing bibliometric analysis. information visit https://rubenfcasal.github.io/scimetr/articles/scimetr.html.

title: scimetr: Analysis of Scientific Publication Data with R — scimetr-package
text: Maintainer: Ruben Fernandez-Casal rubenfcasal@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Borja Lafuente-Rego borjalrego@gmail.com (ORCID) Maria Jose Lombardia maria.jose.lombardia@udc.es (ORCID) Javier Tarrio-Saavedra javier.tarrio@udc.es (ORCID) Julian Costa-Bouzas julian.costa.bouzas@udc.es (ORCID) contributors: Yesica Fernandez-Ramos yesica.ramos@udc.es [contributor] Guillermo Lopez-Taboada guillermo.lopez.taboada@udc.es [contributor]

what: Summaries of a bibliographic database — summary.wos.db
title: Summaries of a bibliographic database — summary.wos.db
text: summary() methods bibliometric databases.

title: Summaries of a bibliographic database — summary.wos.db
code: # S3 method for class 'wos.db' summary(object, filter, index = c("H", "G"), nmax = 10, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.wos.db' print(x, ...)  author_metrics(db, id_authors)  # S3 method for class 'summary.wos.db' plot(   x,   which = 1:5,   pie = FALSE,   others = !pie,   accuracy = 0.1,   plot = TRUE,   ask = length(which) > 1 && interactive(),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'wos.jcr' summary(object, filter, all = FALSE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.wos.jcr' print(x, digits = 2, all = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.wos.jcr' plot(x, plot = TRUE, all = FALSE, ask = plot && all && interactive(), ...)

title: Summaries of a bibliographic database — summary.wos.db
text: object object summary desired. filter vector document identifiers (optional, usually result get_id_docs()). index citation indexes. nmax number top levels. ... arguments passed methods. x object used select method. db Object class wos.db, returned db_bib. id_authors optional; author identifiers subset plots required, specify subset numbers 1:5. pie logical; TRUE, pie charts drawn. others logical; FALSE, nmax top levels categories shown. accuracy numerical scalar; see label_number(accuracy=.). plot logical; TRUE (default), plots drawn, otherwise list ggplot2 objects (invisibly) returned. ask logical; TRUE, user asked plot, see par(ask=.). logical; TRUE, corresponding wos.db method called (additional parameters ... passed ), otherwise results JCR metrics returned. digits minimal number significant digits, see print.default.

title: Summaries of a bibliographic database — summary.wos.db
text: list summary statistics (object class summary.wos.db /summary.wos.jcr), specialized print() plot() methods.

title: Summaries of a bibliographic database — summary.wos.db

title: Summaries per year — summary_year
text: summary_year methods return summaries year bibliometric database.

title: Summaries per year — summary_year
code: summary_year(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'wos.db' summary_year(object, filter, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.year.wos' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.year.wos' plot(   x,   which = 1:3,   boxplot = FALSE,   plot = TRUE,   ask = plot && length(which) > 1 && interactive(),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'wos.jcr' summary_year(object, filter, all = FALSE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.year.jcr' print(x, digits = 3, all = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.year.jcr' plot(x, plot = TRUE, all = FALSE, ask = plot && interactive(), ...)

title: Summaries per year — summary_year
text: object object summary desired. ... arguments passed methods. filter vector document identifiers (optional, usually result get_id_docs). x object used select method. subset plots required, specify subset numbers 1:3. boxplot logical; TRUE, boxplots drawn (plots 2 3). plot logical; TRUE (default), plots drawn, otherwise list ggplot2 objects (invisibly) returned. ask logical; TRUE, user asked plot, see par(ask=.). logical; TRUE, corresponding wos.db method called (additional parameters ... passed ), otherwise results JCR metrics returned. digits minimal number significant digits, see print.default.

title: Summaries per year — summary_year
text: list summary statistics (object class summary.year.wos /summary.year.jcr), specialized print() plot() methods.

what: Mathematics UDC WoS Core Collection 2018-2023 data — wosdf
title: Mathematics UDC WoS Core Collection 2018-2023 data — wosdf
text: data set consists 293 publications corresponding WoS search Affiliation field Universidade da Coruña (UDC) (Affiliation: OG = Universidade da Coruna) research area "Mathematics" (SC = Mathematics), indexed Web Science Core Collection: Science Citation Index Expanded (SCI-EXPANDED). Social Sciences Citation Index (SSCI). Arts & Humanities Citation Index (&HCI). years 2018-2023 (generated using import_wos function).

title: Mathematics UDC WoS Core Collection 2018-2023 data — wosdf
Mathematics UDC WoS Core Collection 2018-2023 data — wosdf
text: data frame 293 rows 48 columns: PT Publication Type AU Author AF Author Full Name TI Article Title Source Title SE Book Series Title BS Book Series Subtitle LA Language DT Document Type C1 Address C3 Affiliation RI Researcher Ids OI ORCID NR Cited Reference Count TC Times Cited, WoS Core Z9 Times Cited, Databases U1 180 Day Usage Count U2 Since 2013 Usage Count PU Publisher PI Publisher City PA Publisher Address SN ISSN EI eISSN BN ISBN J9 Journal Abbreviation JI Journal ISO Abbreviation PD Publication Date PY Publication Year VL Volume Issue PN Part Number SU Supplement SI Special Issue MA Meeting Abstract BP Start Page EP End Page AR Article Number DI DOI D2 Book DOI EA Early Access Date PG Number Pages WC WoS Category Web Science Index SC Research Area HC Highly Cited Status HP Hot Paper Status DA Date Export UT Unique WOS ID

title: Mathematics UDC WoS Core Collection 2018-2023 data — wosdf
text: Clarivate Web Science: https://www.webofscience.com/wos/.

title: scimetr 1.2.0 (2026-03-01)
text: Changes prepare package submission CRAN (Defined global variables utils::globalVariables() avoid check warnings related dplyr usage, updated documentation, urls, package description, dependencies, …). Consistent naming functions using snake_case convention ImportSources.wos() renamed import_wos(). CreateDB() renamed db_bib(). CreateDBJCR() renamed db_jcr(). AddDBJCR() renamed add_jcr(). get.idXxxx() functions renamed get_id_xxxx()idXxxx arguments renamed id_xxxx. TC.authors() renamed author_metrics()TC.index argument renamed index.

title: scimetr 1.1.0 (2025-11-22)
text: Added methods wos.jcr-class objects: plot.wos.jcr(), summary.wos.jcr(), print.summary.wos.jcr(), plot.summary.wos.jcr(), summary.year.jcr(), print.summary.year.jcr(), plot.summary.year.jcr() Added dataset dbjcr. Added functions CreateDBJCR(), AddDBJCR(), get_jcr(), get_jcr_cat(). Added (internal) global variables .jcr.labels ..labels. summary_year() now generic function (methods wos.db wos.jcr classes). Added return value plot methods plot.wos.db(), plot.summary.wos.db() plot.summary.year.wos().

title: scimetr 1.0.0 (2025-03-21)
text: Major internal changes: Changes database structure: new variables, tables, relationships added. NA_character_ used instead "" missing value string variables. New global objects .wos.labels WosCat (stored R/sysdata.rda). New generic function CreateDB() (previous function CreateDB.wos() now method CreateDB.wos.data()). Added arguments idOI, idRI, idAffiliations get.idDocs(). Added functions get.idOI(), get.idRI(), get.idAffiliations(). New results added summary() summary_year(). new dataset wosdf included instead previous ones. Changes match_authors() speed computations.

title: scimetr 0.3.6 (2023-04-21)
text: Changes CreateDB.wos() correct inconsistencies ISSN eISSN WoS downloaded data.

title: scimetr 0.3.5 (2022-09-16)
text: Added NEWS.md (Changelog) Small changes ImportSources.wos() (format changes WoS tab-delimited files). Fixed bug authors.short() (author’s first name included comma). Added check CreateDB.wos() duplicated ORCIDs.

title: scimetr 0.3.4 (2021-11-05)
text: First stable version.

title: scimetr 0.2.3 (2019-09-27)
text: Initial version package form.
